core_mechanism: a boundary-bulk (holographic) duality that computes the linear-response transport coefficients of an intractable strongly-coupled system by mapping it to an equivalent solvable classical field/geometry problem in one higher dimension, with an effective horizon in the higher-dimensional space governing dissipation
brief_reason: The abstract carries out the entire conductivity-tensor and drag-force computation via the AdS/CFT correspondence, solving for a probe brane's worldvolume gauge field in a curved (AdS-Schwarzschild) bulk with an identified effective horizon and reading off the induced current from imposed external fields.
